content:
4 min read
Just now

--

Imagine you have a cool website that shows off who you are and what you can do.. Maybe you want a place to show your work or write about the things you like. Now imagine all of that is on the internet. It does not cost you anything. You do not have to pay every month to keep it up. You do not have to look at ads you do not want to see.. You do not have to worry about making sure everything is working right.
Sounds too good to be true? Then you should check out GitHub Pages.
GitHub Pages is something GitHub offers for free. It takes the code you write. Turns it into a real website. If you are in school or if you like to write code or if you like to write stories putting your website on GitHub is a big deal.
Here is how you can make your website on GitHub Pages. It is easy to follow. You can do it in less, than 10 minutes. GitHub Pages makes it simple.
Why Choose GitHub Pages?
Before we start with the steps let us see why millions of developers use GitHub Pages of traditional hosting platforms.

• GitHub Pages is a choice because it is 100 percent free. There are no costs and no hosting renewals to worry about.
2.Incredibly Fast & Reliable: GitHub Pages is also incredibly fast and reliable. The reason for this is that your site is powered by GitHubs infrastructure. This means that your site loads instantly anywhere in the world.
3. Security Included: GitHub Pages also includes security. GitHub automatically gives you a SSL certificate, for your domain.
4. Customs Domain: You can also use a custom domain with GitHub Pages. For example you can easily link a custom domain like yourname.com to your site on for free.
Step 1: Create a Free GitHub Account
If you do not have a GitHub account you should go to github.com and sign up for a free GitHub account. You need to pick a username that you like because your GitHub username will be part of your websites URL, by default. Your websites URL will include your GitHub username.
Step 2: Create a Special Repository
Once you are logged into GitHub you need to create a storage space for your websites files. This storage space is called a repository.
Click the + icon in the right corner of the screen and select New repository.
The Repository Name is very important.
You need to name your repository, like this:
yourusername.github.io
Replace yourusername with your actual GitHub username.
For example if your username is Sabin123 then your repository name must be Sabin123.github.io.
Set the visibility to Public.
GitHub Pages requires accounts to have public repositories to host sites.
Check the box that says Add a README file.
Scroll down. Click the green Create repository button.
Step 3: Write Your Code (index.html)
Now that you have made your repository you need to add the file for your website. The main page of your website is always called index.html.
Inside your repository page you should click on Add file and then select Create new file.
When you do that you will see a box that says “Name your file…”. You should type index.html in that box.
In the space, below that you can copy and paste the following simple HTML code for your index.html file:
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Scroll to the bottom of the page, click Commit changes (this saves your file into the repository).
Step 4: Turn on GitHub Pages
Now that GitHub has your files you need to tell GitHub to turn on the website features.Your files are safe in the cloud.
0. At the top of your repository page, click on the Settings tab (it has a gear icon next to it).
1.On the left-hand sidebar, look under the “Code and automation” section and click on Pages.
2.Under the Build and deployment section, locate the Branch dropdown menu.
3.Change it from None to main (or master), leaving the folder option as / (root).
4.Click Save.
Step 5: Your Site is Live!
Give GitHub about 1 to 2 minutes to process your files in the background. Refresh your settings page, and at the top of the Pages screen, you will see a success message that looks like this:
Your site is live at https://yourusername.github.io/
Click that link, and your website will open up in a new tab for anyone in the world to see!
Wrapping Up & Next Steps
Congratulations! You have officially bypassed expensive server management tools and published your own space on the web.
Whenever you want to update your website, you can simply edit your index.html file right inside GitHub, or upload new assets like images, stylesheets (style.css), or extra subpages.
Have you tried setting up your own GitHub Pages site yet? If you run into any errors or need a recommendation for beautiful HTML templates to step up your design, drop a comment below!
